title: Dormant Home Country Pattern with Sudden Foreign High-Value Cluster
narrative: A card with 90+ days of consistent domestic transactions in the cardholder's country (e.g., US addr1 prefix) suddenly shows zero activity in that country while generating 8-12 transactions totaling $2,400-$4,800 exclusively in a foreign country within 72 hours. The domestic transaction history shows regular low-value purchases ($15-$85) at grocery/gas MCCs with card1_txn_count_7d typically at 4-6. The foreign burst features elevated amounts ($180-$420 per transaction), all CNP with mismatched R_emaildomain geography, ProductCD shifts from physical goods to digital/services, and dist1 values exceeding 8,000km. Transactions cluster in 6-hour windows during what would be 2AM-5AM in the cardholder's timezone (is_night=1 for home country). No transactions return to the original country even after the foreign cluster ends, suggesting account takeover during travel or credential theft exploiting a genuine trip.
indicators: card1_txn_count_90d shows 35+ transactions, all in cardholder country, then 90d_home_country_count drops to zero
8-12 transactions in 48-72 hours, all from single foreign country with dist1 > 7500km from historical addr1
Foreign transaction amounts $180-$420 each versus historical card1_amt_mean of $45-$75
All foreign transactions during txn_hour 6-12 mapping to 2AM-6AM in cardholder's original timezone (is_night=1 for home country)
R_emaildomain country code mismatches transaction country in 70%+ of foreign transactions
ProductCD switches from 'W' (physical) to 'C'/'H' (digital/services) for 85%+ of foreign cluster
Zero return transactions to original country for 14+ days post-foreign-cluster
summary: A card with 90+ days of steady domestic purchases suddenly goes silent at home while 8-12 high-value transactions appear in a single foreign country within 72 hours, all card-not-present with amounts 3-5x the normal spend and timing that falls in the middle of the night back home.
reasoning: Legitimate travelers typically show overlapping activity or gradual geographic shifts, not an instant switch from one country to another with zero domestic fallback—this abrupt cutover with inflated amounts and graveyard-shift timing signals a fraudster exploiting stolen credentials during or immediately after a genuine trip.
indicator_explanations: Card was active domestically for months then abruptly stops all home-country activity
Burst of transactions suddenly appears from a single distant foreign location
Purchase amounts are triple to quintuple the cardholder's normal spending level
Foreign transactions occur during middle-of-night hours back in cardholder's home timezone
Email domain country doesn't match where transactions are actually occurring
Card shifts from buying physical goods to intangible digital services suddenly
No return to original country activity after foreign spending spree begins
